login.subtitle: Enter your credentials to continue
login.defaultHint: If you have not set up another account: admin / admin123
login.cloudHint: Sign in with the email and password from your administrator. If it is a one-time temporary password, you will be asked to choose your own password after signing in.
login.mustChangePasswordAlert: Your account is using a one-time temporary password. Choose a new password to continue.
login.noCommerceAssigned: Your account has no commerce assigned yet. Contact support or submit a demo request.
login.cloudUnavailable: This build is not linked to email sign-in. Please contact support.
login.cloudLoginRejected: Incorrect email or password. If you pasted the password, try without leading or trailing spaces.
login.apiNotReady: The app is not ready yet. Reload the page or try again later.
login.emailRequired: Please enter a valid email address.

licenseGate.activateFooter: Don't have your key? Contact support.
licenseGate.adminHint: If you are an administrator, sign in with your email and password on the login screen.
licenseGate.blockNoConfig: We could not verify the license. Please contact support.

demoOnboarding.title: Request a demo
demoOnboarding.subtitle: Fill in the form. We will contact you with next steps.
demoOnboarding.successDesc: We will review your details and reply by email.
demoOnboarding.noApi: Supabase is not configured in this build (VITE_SUPABASE_URL / VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY).

ventas.priceCheckHint: F3 to open/close · ESC to close
ventas.selectClienteWarning: Select a customer to charge to account

productos.title: Products
productos.scannerReady: USB scanner ready — scan the barcode
productos.similarQuestion: Product "{{nombre}}" already exists with stock {{stock}}. Add stock to existing or create new?
productos.bulkCategoryExtra: Leave empty to apply to all products

stock.lowStockAlert: {{count}} product(s) with low or depleted stock
stock.expiryExtra: Optional — automatically updates the product alert

caja.title: Cash Register
caja.noCajaAlert: No open register. Open the register to record sales and movements.
caja.closeWarning: This will close today's register. You will not be able to register more movements.

usuarios.title: Users
usuarios.adminOnly: Only administrators can create and modify users.
usuarios.cannotDeleteSelf: You cannot delete your own user

configuracion.title: Settings
configuracion.commerceDataSubtitle: This data appears on sales receipts.
configuracion.ticketFooterPlaceholder: E.g.: Thank you for your purchase!
configuracion.catDeleteDesc: Can only be deleted if it has no associated products.
configuracion.catEmpty: No categories. Create the first one.
configuracion.demoDescription: Load sample products, categories and suppliers to demonstrate the system to a client. Clearing only removes demo data (real data is not affected).
configuracion.clearDemoDesc: Demo products, categories and suppliers will be deleted.
configuracion.loadDemoSuccess: Demo loaded: {{creados}} products, {{categorias}} categories, {{proveedores}} suppliers ({{omitidos}} already existed)

backup.title: Database Backup
backup.statAutoDesc: Runs on app startup if more than 24h have passed
backup.alertDanger: No backup has been made in over 48h. Create one now.
backup.alertNever: No backups registered. We recommend creating one now.
backup.restoreConfirmDesc: The current state will be saved as backup before restoring.
backup.restoreWarning: A backup of the current state will be made before restoring. The app will close to apply changes.
backup.restoreSuccess: Backup restored. Restart the application to see the changes.
backup.restoreQuestion: Are you sure you want to restore the backup from {{date}}?
backup.pwaInfoDesc: Download a JSON file with all data stored on this device (IndexedDB). To restore, import a file created by this app. The page reloads after import.
backup.pwaImportConfirmBody: The current database in this browser will be cleared and replaced with the file. This cannot be undone.
backup.pwaNoFileList: There is no .db file list on the web; use export/import JSON above.
backup.jsonSnapshotDesc: The same file works in the desktop app (SQLite) and in the browser (IndexedDB). After import the app reloads; close other windows if open.
backup.syncNotConfiguredDesc: Set `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` and `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` in the PWA build to enable product synchronization.
backup.syncInfoDesc: This phase only syncs the products table using `commerce_id`. For production multi-tenant use, strong authentication or an intermediate backend is still required before exposing it to end customers.
backup.cloudLoginDesc: Sign in with a Supabase Auth user linked to this commerce. That session is used to enforce RLS policies during sync.
backup.cloudCommerceMismatchTitle: Commerce ID not linked to cloud session
backup.cloudCommerceMismatchDesc: Local `commerce_id` is {{local}}, but this session only has access to: {{list}}. Link the user in `user_commerces` or use the correct commerce.

licencias.title: Licenses
licencias.demoHintShort: In this section you can copy and send access details to the client (user, temporary password, and key).
licencias.demoHint: “Provision” handles web-form requests; “Manual signup” creates access without a prior request. Notes store the password to copy to the client (plain text). It is a one-time temporary password: on first PWA sign-in the user must set a permanent password. If the Auth user already existed and you did not set a password here, reset it in Supabase or use their existing password.
licencias.demoAltasSplitDescription: The table above only lists public form submissions and manual signups (commerce + Supabase user). If you created a demo with “New license” on the Licenses tab, there will be no row here—use the table below for the GCOM key, commerce ID, and notes.
licencias.demoDirectSectionExtra: Same data as on the Licenses tab, grouped here to copy to the client. Email and login password are not included unless you added them in Notes.
licencias.demoDirectEmpty: No demo licenses yet. Create one with “New license” or use “Manual signup” above.
licencias.paidSectionExtra: This block helps review paid clients without mixing with demo onboarding and credential delivery.
licencias.mailSkippedResend: Provisioning succeeded but no email was sent: set RESEND_API_KEY on your deployment server (and a verified sender in Resend). You can still copy credentials from the panel.
licencias.demoPasswordHint: One-time temporary password; the user will set their own on first sign-in. If empty, the backend generates one automatically.
licencias.leadsHint: This table includes both upgrade requests (upgrade_requests) and demo requests submitted from the public form. Demo rows include a “Provision” button.
licencias.demoUrlMissingDesc: For the PWA build, set VITE_PUBLIC_DEMO_URL in .env (your public app URL). In the desktop app you can use resources/client.json → publicDemoUrl instead.
licencias.fieldEsDemoHint: Mark as demo to tell trials apart from paying customers in your panel (expiry is still controlled by the date below).
licencias.fieldCommercePlaceholder: e.g. demo-store-1 or pick from list
licencias.fieldCommerceIdHint: Should match a row in public.commerces if you want multi-tenant sync aligned. You can leave it empty and use only the GCOM key.
licencias.commerceEmpty: No commerces in Supabase. Create one in the SQL Editor (insert into commerces).
licencias.createdDesc: Send this key to the client. It is only shown once.

comercios.title: Stores / tenants
comercios.alertDesc: The license key controls the demo period and modules in the app. Cloud email and password are Supabase Auth credentials: they are only used to sync data (e.g. products) when you enable the session in Backup.
comercios.intro: Recommended flow for onboarding a new store:
comercios.step1Desc: Under Licenses you create the row in Supabase, expiry date and modules. You get the GCOM-… key for the client.
comercios.step2Desc: The client enters the key on the first screen (or settings, depending on your flow). The store is then tied to that license.
comercios.step3Desc: If you use multi-tenant, in Backup the user signs in to the cloud and you verify the local commerce_id matches user_commerces in Supabase.

ayuda.title: Help & User Manual
ayuda.subtitle: Complete guide for using the commerce management system.

productos.title: Products
productos.step1New: Go to the side menu → "Products" → "New Product" button.
productos.step2New: Fill in the product name. The system suggests existing products as you type.
productos.step3New: If a similar product is detected, you'll be asked whether to add stock to the existing one or create a new one.
productos.step4New: Fill in cost price, sale price, category, supplier, unit and initial stock.
productos.step5New: Click "Save". The initial stock is recorded in the movement history.
productos.tipNew: Initial stock can only be entered when creating the product. To add more stock later, use the "Add stock" field when editing.
productos.scanAlert: The USB scanner activates automatically when the product form is opened.
productos.step1Scan: Open the "New Product" or "Edit Product" form.
productos.step2Scan: Point the scanner at the product barcode and scan.
productos.step3Scan: The code fills in automatically in the "Barcode" field. The border turns green as confirmation.
productos.step4Scan: Fill in the remaining fields normally.
productos.tipScan: If the scanner doesn't respond, make sure the form is open and focused. The scanner works like a keyboard — no special driver needed.
productos.step3Manual: Type the code manually and press Enter or Tab.
productos.panelEdit: Edit a product or add stock
productos.step1Edit: In the product list, click the edit icon (pencil) for the product.
productos.step2Edit: Modify the fields you need (price, category, supplier, etc.).
productos.step3Edit: To add stock, use the "Add stock" field and enter the quantity to add. It is added to the current stock on save.
productos.step4Edit: If the product has an expiry, entering a quantity in "Add stock" shows the "Lot expiry date" field to record the new lot's date.
productos.tipEdit: The "Current Stock" field is disabled when editing — you can only increase it using "Add stock". This ensures the movement history is recorded correctly.
productos.expiryIntro: For perishable products (food, medicines, cosmetics, etc.) you can configure an expiry date and alert threshold per product.
productos.step1Expiry: When creating or editing a product, fill in the "Expiry date" field with the current lot's date.
productos.step2Expiry: Once the date is entered, the "Alert days before expiry" field appears (default 7 days). It defines how many days in advance you want the system to alert about expiry.
productos.step3Expiry: On save, the Dashboard will show this product in the "Expiring Soon" panel when the date is within the configured threshold.
productos.step4Expiry: The "Expiry" column in the product list shows the status with colors: grey (more than 30 days), orange (≤ 30 days), red (≤ 7 days or already expired).
productos.tipExpiry: If you don't enter an expiry date, the field is ignored. This feature is completely optional.
productos.panelLots: Managing lots with different expiry dates
productos.lotsIntro: When you receive a new lot of the same product with a different expiry date, the system records each receipt with its own date and always keeps the alert on the most urgent lot.
productos.step1Lots: Edit the product and use the "Add stock" field.
productos.step2Lots: The "Lot expiry date" field appears — enter the new lot's date.
productos.step3Lots: On save, the system automatically updates the product date to the lot that expires first.
productos.lotsExample: Example: you have milk with lot A (expires 04/15) and lot B arrives (expires 04/30). The system saves both in history and keeps the alert on 04/15. When lot A runs out, the next receipt will update the alert to 04/30.
productos.step1Delete: In the list, click the delete icon (red trash) for the product.
productos.step2Delete: Confirm the action in the dialog that appears.

ventas.title: Sales
ventas.step2: Search for the product by name, code or scan the barcode with the USB scanner.
ventas.step3: The product is added to the cart. You can adjust the quantity directly in the cart.
ventas.step4: To apply a discount, enter it in the corresponding field (fixed amount or percentage).
ventas.step5: Select the payment method: cash, card, transfer, Mercado Pago, etc.
ventas.step6: Confirm the sale. The system deducts stock automatically.
ventas.step2Change: The "Amount received" field appears below the payment method.
ventas.step3Change: Enter the amount given by the customer.
ventas.step4Change: The system shows the change in real time: green if sufficient, red if insufficient.
ventas.tipChange: The change field is informational only — it does not affect the sale record.
ventas.panelExpiry: Products expiring soon in the sale
ventas.expiryText: If a product has an expiry date and is expiring soon or already expired, the system shows a warning in the point of sale product list.
ventas.expiryAlert: If you add an already expired product to the cart, the system shows a warning but does not block the sale. You decide whether to continue (e.g., with a discount).
ventas.step1Scan: With the Sales screen open, scan the product barcode.
ventas.step2Scan: If the product is in the system, it is automatically added to the cart.
ventas.step3Scan: Scanning the same code again adds one more unit.
ventas.tipScan: The USB scanner works directly on the sales screen without needing to click any field first.
ventas.step1Ticket: When the sale is completed, the system offers to print the receipt.
ventas.step2Ticket: The ticket includes: business data, product details, subtotal, discount, total and payment method.
ventas.tipTicket: Business data (name, address, phone, footer message) is configured in Settings → receipt data.
ventas.shortcutsIntro: The point of sale can be operated entirely from the keyboard for faster checkout.
ventas.panelPriceCheck: Check a price without adding to cart
ventas.step1PriceCheck: Press F3 or click the "Price Check" button in the top bar.
ventas.step2PriceCheck: Type the product name or code in the panel's search box.
ventas.step3PriceCheck: Results show name, code, category, price, stock and expiry if applicable.
ventas.step4PriceCheck: Close with Escape or by clicking outside the panel.
ventas.tipPriceCheck: Price check is informational only — nothing is added to the cart or stock.
ventas.step1ExportHistory: Go to the "Sales History" tab in the Sales module.
ventas.step2ExportHistory: Click the "Export Excel" button above the table.
ventas.step3ExportHistory: A .xlsx file with the full history is downloaded.
ventas.tipExportHistory: You can also export the inventory and stock movements from the Stock module, and the product list from the Products module.

cotizaciones.title: Quotes
cotizaciones.step3: Search and add products just like a sale.
cotizaciones.step4: You can apply discounts per item or on the total.
cotizaciones.step5: Save the quote. It does not deduct stock.
cotizaciones.panelConvert: Convert a quote to a sale
cotizaciones.step1Convert: Open the quote from the list.
cotizaciones.step3Convert: Confirm the payment method and finish.
cotizaciones.tipConvert: When converting a quote to a sale, stock is deducted and the quote is marked as "Sold".

stock.title: Stock & Inventory
stock.step2View: The table shows all products with their current stock, minimum and status.
stock.step3View: Red products have 0 stock. Orange ones are below the configured minimum.
stock.step2Lot: Select the product, enter the quantity and reason.
stock.step3Lot: If the lot has an expiry date, fill in the "Lot expiry date" field.
stock.step4Lot: Confirm. The movement is recorded with the lot date.
stock.tipLot: When adding a lot with expiry, the product automatically updates its alert to the most urgent date among all registered lots.
stock.historyText: Each inbound, outbound, adjustment or sale is recorded in the history with: date, user, reason and — when applicable — the lot's expiry date. The "Lot expiry" column in the history shows with colors whether the lot is expired (red), expiring soon (orange) or current (grey).
stock.alertsText: On the Dashboard, the "Low Stock" panel shows products with stock equal to or less than the configured minimum. Clicking the panel or "View all" opens the full list with current stock and minimum for each product.

caja.title: Cash Register
caja.step2Open: If no register is open, click "Open Register".
caja.step3Open: Enter the opening amount (cash in register at start of day).
caja.step4Open: Confirm. From that point, cash sales are recorded in this register.
caja.step1Close: Go to the menu → "Cash Register" with the register open.
caja.step3Close: The system shows the summary: opening amount, cash sales, expected total.
caja.step4Close: Enter the actual counted amount and confirm the close.
caja.tipClose: The difference between expected and counted is recorded in the register close history.

proveedores.title: Suppliers
proveedores.step2: Fill in name, contact, phone, email and address.
proveedores.step3: Save. Suppliers are available to assign to products.
proveedores.tip: Assigning a supplier to a product lets you easily filter and sort the catalog by supplier.

reportes.title: Reports
reportes.step2: Select the date range you want to analyze.
reportes.step3: The system shows: total sales, number of transactions, top products and daily evolution.
reportes.item3: Number of products with low stock (clickable → opens the full list)
reportes.item4: Sales chart for the last 7 days
reportes.item5: Low Stock panel: lists products with stock alerts. Click "View all" for details.
reportes.item6: Expiring Soon panel: shows products whose expiry date is within the configured alert threshold. Click "View all" for full details with exact dates.
reportes.tipDashboard: The "Expiring Soon" panel only appears when there are products with an expiry date. If no products have expiry configured, the panel shows "No expiry alerts".

usuarios.title: Users
usuarios.step2: Fill in name, username (for login) and password.
usuarios.step3: Assign the role: Administrator or Seller.

configuracion.title: Settings
configuracion.step2: Fill in: business name, address, phone, tax ID and receipt footer message.
configuracion.step3: This data appears on all printed receipts and documents.
configuracion.categoriesText: Product categories are managed directly from Settings, in the right column of the screen.
configuracion.step2Cat: In the "Product Categories" section click "New Category".
configuracion.step3Cat: Enter the name and an optional description.
configuracion.step4Cat: Save. The category is immediately available in the product form.
configuracion.catAlert: To delete a category, you must first reassign or delete the products that use it. The system does not allow deleting a category with associated products.

backup.title: Backup & Restore
backup.step3: Select the folder where to save the file.
backup.step4: The generated .db file contains all database information.
backup.backupWarning: We recommend making a daily backup and storing it externally (USB drive, Google Drive, etc.).
backup.step4Restore: Confirm. The application restarts with the restored data.
backup.restoreError: Restoring a backup replaces all current data. This action cannot be undone.

clientes.title: Customers
clientes.priceListExtra: Default price for sales to this customer

listasPrecio.title: Price Lists
listasPrecio.removeItemConfirm: Remove this product from the list?
